title: Piaradena fulgens
taxonomy: Piaradena fulgens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to Brazil and is also known by its synonym Psychotria fulgens. The classification of Piaradena fulgens is still under review, with some sources considering it a synonym of Psychotria fulgens and others recognizing it as a distinct species. Further research is needed to clarify the classification of Piaradena fulgens. The plant is often found in humid, tropical environments, such as rainforests and cloud forests.

intro: Piaradena fulgens, commonly known as the fire-flower or scarlet fire-flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Piaradena fulgens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Piaradena fulgens is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
